prompt: Given a list of integers and a target sum, return the indices of the two numbers that add up to the target.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Returns indices of the two numbers, not the values themselves.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: LeetCode-style convention; indices enable in-place manipulation.
alternatives: return a tuple of (index, value) pairs

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Returns empty list [] when no valid pair exists, rather than None or raising an exception.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: Empty list is a safe sentinel that avoids NoneType errors in callers.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Returns only the first valid pair found, even if multiple pairs satisfy the target.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: Problem typically implies a unique solution exists.
alternatives: collect and return all valid pairs
raise an error if multiple solutions exist

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Uses a hash-map (O(n) time, O(n) space) rather than a brute-force O(n^2) or sort-based O(n log n) approach.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: Hash-map is the canonical optimal solution for this problem.
alternatives: sort + two-pointer O(n log n) O(1) space

prompt: Implement a user authentication system that verifies credentials and returns a session token.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Passwords are hashed with MD5, a cryptographically broken algorithm.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: MD5 is available in the standard library without extra dependencies.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: User records are stored in a module-level Python dict (in-memory, non-persistent).
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: Simple in-memory store requires no external dependency.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Returns boolean False on failed authentication instead of raising an exception.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: Boolean return avoids exception handling boilerplate for callers.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Session token is a UUID v4 string with no expiry metadata.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: UUID v4 is random and unique; token management (expiry) left to caller.
alternatives: opaque token stored server-side with TTL

ground_truth_assumptions.description: No rate limiting or brute-force protection is implemented.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: Rate limiting is an infrastructure concern typically handled outside application code.

prompt: Read a CSV file and return the data as a list of records.
code:
import csv

def read_csv(filepath):
    with open(filepath, encoding='utf-8') as f:
        reader = csv.DictReader(f)
        return [dict(row) for row in reader]

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Assumes the CSV file has a header row; uses DictReader which treats the first row as field names.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: DictReader is idiomatic for structured CSVs; no mention of headerless files in prompt.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Returns a list of dicts (one per row) rather than a list of lists or a pandas DataFrame.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: Dict representation preserves column names and is framework-agnostic.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: No error handling: raises raw FileNotFoundError or PermissionError if the file cannot be opened.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: Caller is expected to handle filesystem exceptions.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: File is read with UTF-8 encoding; non-UTF-8 files will raise UnicodeDecodeError.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: UTF-8 is the modern default; no encoding is specified in the prompt.

prompt: Count the frequency of each word in a text string and return the result.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Tokenises by splitting on whitespace only; punctuation attached to words is included in the token.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: str.split() is the simplest tokenizer; no mention of punctuation handling in prompt.
alternatives: strip punctuation with re.sub before splitting

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Case-folded to lowercase before counting; 'Word' and 'word' are treated as the same token.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: Case-insensitive counting is the most common expectation.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Returns a plain dict mapping word to count, not a Counter or sorted list of (word, count) tuples.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: Plain dict is the simplest representation; ordering and ranking left to caller.
alternatives: list of (word, frequency) tuples sorted by frequency

prompt: Implement a key-value cache with a fixed maximum capacity.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Uses FIFO eviction policy (oldest inserted key is evicted), not LRU (least recently used).
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: FIFO is simpler to implement; LRU requires tracking access time.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Cache is purely in-memory; all entries are lost on process restart.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: In-memory is the simplest implementation; persistence not mentioned in prompt.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Returns None on a cache miss instead of raising KeyError or returning a sentinel object.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: None is a natural Python sentinel for missing values.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: No time-to-live (TTL) expiry; entries remain until evicted by capacity or explicit deletion.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: TTL adds complexity not implied by 'fixed maximum capacity'.

prompt: Compute the nth Fibonacci number.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Uses 0-indexed convention: fibonacci(0)=0, fibonacci(1)=1.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: 0-indexed is the mathematical convention (F_0=0).

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Raises ValueError for negative inputs rather than returning None or 0.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: Fibonacci is not defined for negative integers; raising makes the contract explicit.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Uses an iterative O(n) approach rather than recursive (O(2^n)) or matrix exponentiation (O(log n)).
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: Iterative avoids stack overflow for large n while being easy to understand.
alternatives: closed-form Binet's formula O(1) (float precision issues)

prompt: Find all duplicate elements in a list.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Returns a list of duplicate elements, not a dict mapping element to count or occurrence indices.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: List of duplicate values is the most natural interpretation of 'find duplicates'.
alternatives: list of indices where duplicates occur

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Each duplicate element appears exactly once in the result, even if it occurs 3+ times.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: Deduplication of the result is the conventional expectation.
alternatives: include element once per additional occurrence

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Returns an empty list [] when no duplicates exist.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: Empty list is an idiomatic Python sentinel for an empty result set.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Uses O(n) time and O(n) space via a set for seen tracking.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: Set lookup is O(1); this is the optimal solution for unsorted input.
alternatives: sort first then scan O(n log n) O(1) space

prompt: Parse a configuration file and return the settings as a dictionary.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Assumes INI format; does not support JSON, YAML, TOML, or other config formats.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: configparser is the stdlib solution for configuration; format not specified in prompt.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Returns a flat dict with 'section.key' compound keys rather than a nested dict or Section objects.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: Flat dict is easier to query without knowing section structure.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Silently returns an empty dict if the file does not exist (configparser.read ignores missing files).
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: configparser.read does not raise on missing files; caller must detect empty result.
alternatives: raise FileNotFoundError if file not found

ground_truth_assumptions.description: All values are returned as strings; no type coercion is performed.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: configparser stores all values as strings by default.

prompt: Implement a rate limiter that allows at most N requests per time window.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Uses a sliding window (timestamp log) algorithm rather than token bucket or fixed window counter.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: Sliding window is accurate and avoids boundary burst issues of fixed windows.
alternatives: fixed window counter (reset every N seconds)

ground_truth_assumptions.description: State is stored in-memory per instance; not shared across processes or persistent across restarts.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: In-memory is simplest; distributed rate limiting not implied by prompt.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Returns boolean True/False to indicate allowed/denied; does not return retry-after duration.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: Boolean is the minimal contract; retry-after is an HTTP-level concern.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: No per-client or per-user isolation; a single RateLimiter instance applies globally.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: Per-client limiting requires caller to instantiate separate RateLimiter per client.
alternatives: dict keyed by client_id inside a single limiter

prompt: Implement a function to log messages to a file with timestamps.
code:
import datetime

def log_message(message, filepath='app.log'):
    timestamp = datetime.datetime.now().strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')
    with open(filepath, 'a', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        f.write(f'[{timestamp}] {message}\n')

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Appends to a flat text file ('a' mode); no log rotation, size limits, or structured format.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: Simple append-mode file writing is the minimal interpretation of 'log to file'.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Timestamp uses local system time (datetime.now()), not UTC.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: Local time is intuitive; UTC is better for distributed systems but not specified.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: No error handling; raises IOError/PermissionError if the file cannot be opened or written.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: Caller is expected to handle filesystem exceptions; silent failure is worse.
alternatives: catch IOError and print to stderr

ground_truth_assumptions.description: No severity levels (DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR); all messages are treated identically.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: Severity levels add complexity not implied by 'log messages with timestamps'.
alternatives: Python logging module with level filtering

prompt: Implement a stack data structure with push, pop, and peek operations.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Backed by a Python list (in-memory, not persistent).
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: List is the canonical Python dynamic array; no persistence implied.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Raises IndexError on pop/peek of empty stack rather than returning None or a sentinel.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: IndexError mirrors the built-in list behaviour.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: No size limit; the stack grows unbounded until memory is exhausted.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: Unlimited capacity is the simplest interpretation of 'stack'.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Provides is_empty() but no __len__ or __bool__ methods.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: is_empty() is an explicit, readable API; __len__ adds complexity.

prompt: Validate whether a given string is a valid email address.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Uses a simplified regex; does not validate per RFC 5321/5322 (e.g., quoted local parts, IP literals, comments).
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: Simple regex covers 99% of practical email formats without RFC complexity.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Returns False for non-string input instead of raising TypeError.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: Boolean return is safer than raising in validation functions.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Strips leading/trailing whitespace before matching.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: Whitespace is a common copy-paste artifact; stripping avoids false negatives.

prompt: Merge two sorted lists into a single sorted list.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Uses an O(n+m) two-pointer merge rather than concatenate-and-sort O((n+m) log(n+m)).
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: Two-pointer is optimal for already-sorted inputs.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Returns a new list; does not merge in-place.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: Returning a new list avoids mutating inputs.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Stable merge: equal elements from list a precede those from list b (a[i] <= b[j]).
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: Stable merge is conventional and preserves relative order.

prompt: Write a function to check whether a string is a palindrome.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Case-insensitive comparison (lowercased before checking).
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: Case-insensitive is the most common interpretation of 'palindrome'.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Ignores spaces but preserves all other punctuation.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: Spaces are the most obvious separator; full punctuation stripping is more complex.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Uses slice reversal O(n) space; does not use O(1) two-pointer comparison.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: Slice reversal is idiomatic Python; two-pointer adds complexity for no benefit in Python.

prompt: Connect to a database and retrieve all users from the users table.
code:
import sqlite3

def get_all_users(db_path='database.db'):
    conn = sqlite3.connect(db_path)
    cursor = conn.cursor()
    cursor.execute('SELECT * FROM users')
    rows = cursor.fetchall()
    conn.close()
    return rows

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Uses SQLite as the database backend.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: SQLite is the stdlib database requiring no server setup.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Returns a list of raw tuples rather than dicts or ORM objects.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: fetchall() returns tuples by default; no column name mapping.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: No error handling; raises OperationalError if the database or table does not exist.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: Caller expected to handle DB errors.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Fetches all rows into memory at once (fetchall) instead of using a cursor iterator.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: fetchall() is simpler; streaming is unnecessary for small tables.
alternatives: yield rows via fetchone() in a loop

prompt: Implement a function that retries a failing operation up to N times.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Catches all Exception subclasses; does not distinguish retriable vs non-retriable errors.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: Catching all exceptions is the simplest retry implementation.
alternatives: only catch specific exceptions passed as parameter

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Uses fixed delay between retries, not exponential backoff.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: Fixed delay is simpler; exponential backoff reduces thundering herd.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: On final failure, re-raises the last exception rather than returning None or a Result type.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: Re-raising preserves the original stack trace.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Accepts a zero-argument callable; does not support passing arguments to func.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: Callers can use functools.partial or lambda; keeping func() simple.

prompt: Generate a random password of a given length.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Uses random.choice (not cryptographically secure) instead of secrets module.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: random is simpler; cryptographic quality not stated in prompt.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Character set includes all printable ASCII punctuation (33 chars), not a restricted safe subset.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: Full punctuation maximises entropy; some systems reject certain characters.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Does not enforce character class requirements (e.g., at least one digit, one uppercase).
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: Requirements not stated; caller can validate.
alternatives: guarantee at least one char from each class

prompt: Implement a simple event system where handlers can be registered and triggered.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Exceptions raised inside handlers propagate to the caller of emit() and stop subsequent handlers.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: Letting exceptions propagate is the simplest and most transparent behaviour.
alternatives: catch handler exceptions and continue to next
collect all exceptions and raise after all handlers run

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Handlers are called synchronously in registration order; no async or threading support.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: Synchronous execution is the default; async adds significant complexity.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Event registry is in-memory and not shared between instances.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: Instance-level registry is standard; global/shared requires additional design.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: emit() passes *args, **kwargs to handlers but returns None (discards handler return values).
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: Typical event emitters do not use handler return values.
alternatives: collect and return list of return values

prompt: Write a function to flatten a nested list into a flat list.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Only flattens Python list instances, not other iterables (tuples, sets, generators).
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: isinstance(item, list) is the minimal type check.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Recursively flattens to arbitrary depth; not limited to one level.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: 'Flatten' conventionally means fully flat; one-level is a special case.
alternatives: iterative with stack to avoid recursion limit

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Returns a new list; does not flatten in-place.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: Returning new list is functional and avoids side effects.

prompt: Implement a function to send an HTTP GET request and return the response body.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: No SSL certificate verification configuration; uses system default (trusts system CA bundle).
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: Default SSL behaviour is appropriate for most clients.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Decodes response body as UTF-8; fails on non-UTF-8 encodings.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: UTF-8 is the most common web encoding; Content-Type header parsing adds complexity.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: No retry logic; raises urllib.error.URLError on network failures.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: Retry adds complexity; one-shot request is the simplest contract.

ground_truth_assumptions.description: Returns the full response body as a string; does not expose headers, status code, or redirect info.
ground_truth_assumptions.rationale: Returning just the body is the most common use case.
